summary: P0 Dashboard Click-Through & Pool Access Control Testing Complete - All features verified and working. Backend APIs (19/19 tests passed) and Frontend UI (all components verified). Fixed bug in auth.py where can_pick and pool_access fields were missing from /auth/me response.
passed_tests: Backend: Admin login successful
Backend: Media Client login successful
Backend: Admin can access Pool 1 tickets
Backend: Admin can access Pool 2 tickets
Backend: Media Client blocked from Pool 1 (403 Forbidden)
Backend: User with can_pick=false blocked from pools
Backend: User with pool_access=pool1 blocked from Pool 2
Backend: User with pool_access=pool2 blocked from Pool 1
Backend: User with pool_access=both can access all pools
Backend: Dashboard v2 metrics API returns KPI data
Backend: Orders filter by status works (/orders?status=Open)
Backend: My-requests endpoint works for Media Clients
Backend: Admin can update user pool_access setting
Backend: Setting can_pick=false automatically sets pool_access=none
Backend: Invalid pool_access values are rejected (400)
Backend: User response includes can_pick and pool_access fields
Backend: /auth/me response includes can_pick and pool_access fields (FIXED)
Frontend: Admin lands on dashboard after login
Frontend: Admin sees Opportunity Ribbon in sidebar
Frontend: Admin dashboard shows Pool Status widgets
Frontend: Dashboard KPI click-through works (Open → /orders?status=Open)
Frontend: Media Client lands on dashboard after login
Frontend: Media Client does NOT see Opportunity Ribbon in sidebar
Frontend: Media Client click-through to /my-tickets works
Frontend: IAM Add User form shows 'Can pick opportunities from pools' toggle
Frontend: IAM Add User form shows 'Pool Access Level' dropdown when can_pick=ON
Frontend: IAM Edit User form shows pool access controls
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed bug: auth.py UserResponse model was missing can_pick and pool_access fields
Pool access control is properly enforced at API level with 403 responses
Dashboard KPI cards are wrapped with Link components for click-through navigation
Layout.js correctly filters Opportunity Ribbon based on can_pick and pool_access
IAMPage.js shows Pool Access Level dropdown only when can_pick toggle is ON
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing test users
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 Dashboard Click-Through and Pool Access Control features are complete and working. Key points: 1) Dashboard KPI cards are clickable and navigate to filtered views. 2) Pool visibility respects can_pick and pool_access settings. 3) Media Clients do NOT see Opportunity Ribbon. 4) IAM page shows Pool Access Level dropdown when can_pick is ON. 5) Fixed bug in auth.py where can_pick and pool_access were missing from /auth/me response.

Dashboard_Click_Through_Admin.details: Admin clicks 'Open' KPI card → navigates to /orders?status=Open

Dashboard_Click_Through_Media_Client.details: Media Client clicks KPI → navigates to /my-tickets with filter

Pool_Access_Control_can_pick_false.details: User with can_pick=false gets 403 Forbidden from Pool API

Pool_Access_Control_pool_access_pool1.details: User with pool_access=pool1 gets 403 from Pool 2 API

Sidebar_Visibility_Opportunity_Ribbon.details: User with can_pick=false does NOT see Opportunity Ribbon in sidebar

IAM_Pool_Access_Dropdown.details: Pool Access Level dropdown appears when can_pick toggle is ON

Default_Landing_Dashboard.details: After login, user lands on dashboard (root /)

bugs_fixed.issue: UserResponse model missing can_pick and pool_access fields
bugs_fixed.fix: Added can_pick: bool = True and pool_access: str = 'both' to UserResponse model and build_user_response function

mocked_apis.note: GoHighLevel payment webhook is MOCKED as per previous context
